---
title: "Clinical Trial Omics"
subtitle: "Treatment cohorts and trial-linked omics datasets"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
---

<div class="page-intro">
This page separates treatment-response and clinical-trial-linked omics from observational cohorts and broad consortia. Trial names, interventions, longitudinal status, and response-readiness are registry-level annotations and should be checked against the source before formal analysis.
</div>

## Trial and Treatment Cohorts

<div id="clinical-trial-omics"></div>

---

## Interpretation Notes

- Trial-linked omics are often the most valuable datasets for translational work, but response definitions, sampling windows, placebo/comparator structure, and post-treatment timepoints vary by study.
- A dataset can be useful for treatment-response biology even if it is not a randomized trial.
- Rows with unclear treatment metadata are retained for review rather than excluded.

<div class="disclaimer">
<strong>Use carefully.</strong> Do not pool trial cohorts until treatment class, timepoint, endpoint definition, specimen type, and cohort overlap are checked.
</div>
